title: Two further tranches under the four-year bailout were approved, releasing about $695m.
body_paragraphs: The disbursement lifted total programme flows to roughly $2.4bn and was booked by the Treasury. Policymakers said the cash would bolster reserves while reforms on revenue, state enterprises and social protection continue.

The Board’s green light followed a combined fifth and sixth review of targets under the Extended Fund Facility (a multi‑year programme of policy conditions and financing). Business groups called the decision a confidence signal, even as they urged faster structural change amid external shocks.

title: The current account posted a US$ 532m deficit in April as imports surged.
body_paragraphs: Statistical releases showed the first monthly shortfall since October 2025, driven by a widening trade gap and softer services inflows. Fuel purchases jumped by nearly 150% year‑on‑year to about US$ 886m, while vehicle imports also rose, outpacing a 10.9% rise in merchandise exports.

Tourism receipts fell for a second month as regional tensions curbed arrivals, although remittances rose 18.8% to US$ 768m and helped cushion the external balance. Officials said reserves stayed near US$ 6.8bn, but warned imported energy costs and freight would keep pressure on the external account.

title: The selling quote for the dollar climbed to Rs. 340.25.
body_paragraphs: Daily quotations showed the buying side inching up to Rs. 330.10, with dealers also reporting weakness against the euro, sterling and Swiss franc. Traders cited higher energy outlays and import demand, while noting that exchange‑rate flexibility (a policy of letting the rate move with market conditions) remains part of the reform framework.

Analysts said the move was modest compared with the spike seen during the crisis, but warned sustained oil near US$ 100 could lift the annual fuel bill towards US$ 4.5–5bn. They added that transparency over intervention and pass‑through to prices would help anchor expectations.

title: The Government moved to strictly enforce the fuel QR code, seeking a daily diesel saving of 500 tonnes.
body_paragraphs: Ministers said the import bill had topped US$ 500m a month amid Middle East disruptions, and urged lower consumption after rationing earlier cut usage to about 4,200 tonnes a day. Officials added that options such as remote work for public servants were under review to preserve foreign exchange.

The quota tool, introduced in 2022, is a digital allocation system (QR is a scannable code that links identity to permitted volumes) managed at the pump. Transport groups warned of cost pressures, while the energy ministry pointed to monthly quotes exceeding US$ 630m at the March peak.

title: The Supreme Court set aside a 2021 order that had discharged key counts in the 2016 bond case.
body_paragraphs: The apex bench held that a company can be prosecuted under the Offences Against Public Property Act, reviving charges that a lower tribunal had dropped. The matter stems from a contested auction where prosecutors allege losses to the State and name ten accused, including a primary dealer and former officials.

By allowing the appeal, judges cleared the way for trial on counts 1 to 11 of the indictment. Lawyers said the ruling reaffirmed corporate criminal liability and narrowed a loophole that had impeded complex financial prosecutions.

title: The first shipment of grid‑scale battery units arrived for installation at six substations.
body_paragraphs: The cargo, weighing up to 42 metric tonnes per unit, will be deployed at Mahawa, Vavuniya, Chunnakam, Kilinochchi, Anuradhapura and Polonnaruwa. Each site is designed for 40 MWh of storage and 10 MW discharge, part of a wider Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) programme to firm variable solar and wind.

Energy planners said the fleet will support frequency and voltage services, shift excess daytime generation and cut curtailment. The rollout ties into a 160 MW storage plan aimed at advancing the 70% renewables target by 2030.

title: At least 11 residents died in a blaze at an elders’ home in Horana.
body_paragraphs: Authorities said three people were missing and seven were hospitalised after the fire swept through the facility in Batagoda. Investigators reported a gas cylinder explosion on site and opened inquiries into fire exits, alarms and staffing adequacy.

title: Equities fell for a second day, with benchmark indices down over 1%.
body_paragraphs: The ASPI closed at 21,760.31 and the S&P SL20 at 6,031.73 as selling broadened across 186 counters. Brokers cited geopolitical risks and tariff headlines, while noting crossings of about LKR 0.78 bn helped lift turnover to LKR 2.78 bn.

title: Ten TH‑57 training helicopters were delivered to the Air Force under a US grant.
body_paragraphs: The aircraft, ferried by sea and now undergoing checks at Ratmalana, are to be inducted after inspections and test flights. Planners said the rotary‑wing fleet would support pilot training, search and rescue and disaster‑response duties.

title: The export board moved to expand islandwide testing and certification access.
body_paragraphs: Following a stakeholder forum, officials outlined plans to modernise provincial laboratories, use public‑private partnerships and digitise approvals to cut bottlenecks. The initiative targets exporters outside Colombo who face delays and costs due to centralised facilities.

title: Authorities launched a 2026–2030 anticipatory action plan to link early warning with early response.
body_paragraphs: The roadmap aims to use scientific forecasts to trigger pre‑planned measures before hazards escalate, with a focus on food security, floods and landslides. The Defence Ministry, disaster agencies and UN partners backed the effort to reduce losses through pre‑financed action.
